title: Cyphostemma simulans
taxonomy: Cyphostemma simulans is a species of flowering plant in the grape family (Vitaceae). It belongs to the genus Cyphostemma, which is a group of succulent plants native to sub-Saharan Africa. The genus Cyphostemma is closely related to the genus Cissus, and both are classified within the family Vitaceae. Cyphostemma simulans is further classified as a member of the family Vitaceae and order Vitales. The exact classification may vary slightly depending on the source, but it is generally accepted that Cyphostemma simulans belongs to this family and order.

intro: Cyphostemma simulans, a succulent plant native to the tropical regions of Africa, is a member of the grapevine family (Vitaceae) and is also known as a "living stone" due to its unique ability to store water in its thick, fleshy stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, pain, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
